message:
Use cryptographic random suffix for update temp files

Replace predictable temp file naming with random suffix to mitigate
Time-Of-Check-Time-Of-Use (TOCTOU) attacks during binary updates:

Before: ${currentPath}.new (e.g., /usr/local/bin/jfp.new)
After:  ${currentPath}.update-${randomHex} (e.g., jfp.update-a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8)

Attack scenario prevented:
1. Attacker predicts temp file location
2. Creates symlink or file at that location before download
3. Our download overwrites their controlled file
4. Race between checksum verification and replacement

The 8-byte (64-bit) random suffix makes the filename unpredictable,
requiring the attacker to guess 2^64 possibilities.

Note: This is defense-in-depth - the attack requires write access to
the binary's directory, but unpredictable naming is best practice.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
